title: Shogun Kurozane — Japanese Face and Early-Edo Oni Armor
productionStatus: approved review source; not a pixel-authored runtime asset

revision.reason: Correct the elderly ruler's face to read unmistakably Japanese and replace European-fantasy plate language with recognizable 17th-century Japanese samurai armor and an Oni helmet.
preserved: Black, charcoal, faded-aubergine, old-bronze, and restrained-crimson palette
Neutral concept-board backdrop and paired face studies

creativeBrief.identity: Elderly Japanese vampire shogun with hooded almond eyes, broader cheek structure, lower nasal bridge, warm-ashen complexion, chonmage, thin gray moustache, and sparse chin beard.
creativeBrief.unarmoredRead: Worn early-Edo Japanese kosode and formal layered robes, repaired seams, seal case, slight stoop, and disciplined grief.
creativeBrief.armoredRead: Early-17th-century tosei gusoku / nimai-do construction with black-lacquered iron cuirass, indigo lacing, kusazuri, sode, kote, haidate, suneate, and armored footwear.
creativeBrief.oniHelmetRead: Ribbed iron kawari-kabuto, shikoro neck defense, swept lacquered horns, and an original red-black menpo with a fierce Oni read.
constraints: No resemblance to Peter Cushing, Grand Moff Tarkin, or another real actor
No European facial anatomy, tailoring, cuirass, buckles, or fantasy plate
No authentic clan mon or imperial chrysanthemum
No copied historical crest, inscription, or museum decoration
No copied Noh, Bugaku, ceremonial, sacred, or historical mask
No gore, biological horns, wings, text, logo, or watermark

visualReferences.title: Armor (Nimai-Do Gusoku) with Box
visualReferences.role: period construction, material, lacing, and armor-proportion reference only

visualReferences.title: Helmet (Suji Kabuto)
visualReferences.role: period helmet construction and craftsmanship reference only

nextProductionStep: Derive the approved face into an eight-expression portrait row and the armored silhouette into separately authored boss sprite studies.
